title: The Squirt And The Whale

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer encounters some minor frustration or mishap
jokes.punchline: His signature exclamation of annoyance
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase - perfect character integration and economy, but not particularly original or earned in this context

jokes.text: I love you, Romeo! I love you, Julie-x! Tic-tac-noooo!
jokes.setup: Parody of Romeo and Juliet in a tic-tac-toe movie trailer
jokes.punchline: Julie-x (combining Juliet with X from tic-tac-toe) and the dramatic 'Tic-tac-noooo!'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay combining Shakespeare with tic-tac-toe, good economy but fairly disposable

jokes.text: From the producers of Duck, Duck Goose, Got Your Nose and One Day in the Life of Ivan Denisovich
jokes.setup: Typical movie trailer producer credits format
jokes.punchline: Listing children's games alongside a serious Russian novel
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from silly to serious, but requires knowledge of the Solzhenitsyn novel to fully land

jokes.text: I'm not made of money! I'm made of man-meat and a skeleton!
jokes.setup: Homer complaining about electric bill in typical fashion
jokes.punchline: Literal description of human anatomy instead of the expected metaphor
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer literalism - takes a common expression and makes it absurdly anatomical. Good character voice.

jokes.text: vampire appliances that plunge their prongs into our creamy white outlets to quench their unholy thirst for electrons
jokes.setup: Homer describing energy-consuming appliances
jokes.punchline: Elaborate vampire metaphor with sexual undertones
jokes.explanation: Creative extended metaphor but slightly overwrought. The 'creamy white outlets' adds an unexpected sexual layer

jokes.text: Lamp, computer, Jacob's Ladder...
jokes.setup: Homer listing appliances to unplug
jokes.punchline: Including a torture device/biblical reference among household items
jokes.explanation: Quick throwaway joke - Jacob's Ladder doesn't belong with normal appliances. Economy is excellent

jokes.text: Are you calling me fat? No.
jokes.setup: Lisa suggests lifestyle changes, Homer assumes it's about weight
jokes.punchline: Lisa's deadpan 'No' to his defensive question
jokes.explanation: Homer's insecurity plays well, Lisa's blunt response is funny but predictable

jokes.text: Where there's expos, there's free Frisbees!
jokes.setup: Homer reading about Alternative Energy Expo
jokes.punchline: His motivation is free promotional items, not environmental concern
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - missing the point entirely for trivial rewards

jokes.text: Do they think I'm like an idiot? I'm going to enjoy poong on their carpet.
jokes.setup: Dog is left home alone while family goes out
jokes.punchline: Dog's internal monologue about revenge, using childish word for defecation
jokes.explanation: Mild shock humor with the dog getting internal voice and using euphemism 'poong'

jokes.text: A churro you can eat in the shower?
jokes.setup: Bart says he found what Homer is looking for
jokes.punchline: Homer's completely random and specific desire
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific and absurd Homer desire - combines his love of food with laziness. Great economy.

jokes.text: Goddag, friendlys. Behold to you-- wind!
jokes.setup: Stereotypical foreign salesman introduction
jokes.punchline: Broken English and dramatic presentation of something mundane
jokes.explanation: Relies on accent humor and foreigner stereotypes - fairly cheap comedy

jokes.text: Well, I'm not usually one to take the word of a Dane.
jokes.setup: Danish salesman pitching wind power
jokes.punchline: Homer's arbitrary prejudice against an entire nationality
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer xenophobia but toward a completely harmless country. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: It will pay for itself in 12 to 18... Months? Lifetimes.
jokes.setup: Salesman starting to quote payback period
jokes.punchline: Homer's hopeful 'Months?' followed by crushing 'Lifetimes'
jokes.explanation: Well-structured misdirection with good timing. Homer's optimism crushed efficiently.

jokes.text: Too late. Lars got the twine.
jokes.setup: Homer immediately regretting the wind turbine purchase
jokes.punchline: The irreversible commitment represented by getting twine
jokes.explanation: Absurd logic that getting twine makes the deal irreversible. Good absurdist humor.

jokes.text: Yup, I Al-Gored it pretty good.
jokes.setup: Bart praising Homer for leading in clean energy
jokes.punchline: Using Al Gore's name as a verb for environmental action
jokes.explanation: Decent pun turning a political figure into a verb, though somewhat dated reference

jokes.text: Oh, that's just great. I discover electricity, and they want to take it from me!
jokes.setup: Learning that excess power goes back to the electric company
jokes.punchline: Homer thinking he invented electricity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer delusion - claiming to have discovered something fundamental to civilization

jokes.text: The kite's best friend, the flag's partner in patriotism, you've seen cherubs blow it from map corners-- the wind!
jokes.setup: Dramatic sports announcer introducing tonight's power source
jokes.punchline: Overwrought poetic description of wind
jokes.explanation: Creative metaphors for wind that escalate nicely. Good parody of sports announcing style.

jokes.text: Yeah, blow me some sports!
jokes.setup: Wind-powered sports broadcast announcement
jokes.punchline: Barney's enthusiastic double entendre response
jokes.explanation: Classic Barney inappropriate enthusiasm with sexual innuendo

jokes.text: 'O'er the ramparts we' what?!
jokes.setup: National anthem being sung when power cuts out
jokes.punchline: Crowd's frustration at being left hanging mid-song
jokes.explanation: Relatable frustration about being interrupted during anthem, decent timing

jokes.text: Who turned off the wind?
jokes.setup: Power going out when wind stops
jokes.punchline: Homer treating wind like an appliance someone controls
jokes.explanation: Homer's complete misunderstanding of natural phenomena is perfectly in character

jokes.text: From now on, the Simpsons are living... intermittently.
jokes.setup: Homer declaring independence from big electricity
jokes.punchline: Unintentionally describing their unreliable power situation
jokes.explanation: Good word choice that captures their situation perfectly while Homer remains oblivious

jokes.text: The wind may have stopped working, but my brain nev... ...er does.
jokes.setup: Homer about to claim his brain always works
jokes.punchline: Power outage interrupting him to reveal the opposite truth
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing with the power cut revealing the truth about Homer's intelligence. Well-crafted irony.

jokes.text: But, Dad, it defeats the purpose if you're powering our windmill with the electricity from Flanders.
jokes.setup: Homer has found a solution to the wind power problem
jokes.punchline: He's using Flanders' electricity to power their wind turbine
jokes.explanation: Good irony about defeating the environmental purpose, Lisa as voice of reason

jokes.text: But we haven't read the minutes of last week's meeting!
jokes.setup: Homer declaring fan club meeting adjourned
jokes.punchline: Flanders' earnest desire to follow proper meeting procedure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Flanders character moment - overly formal and earnest about trivial things

jokes.text: I'm trying to hear Dr. House's third incorrect diagnosis before his final correct diagnosis!
jokes.setup: Bart complaining about Lisa interrupting his TV viewing
jokes.punchline: Accurate description of House's predictable formula
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about TV medical drama formula, good meta humor

jokes.text: Dear Lord, you've got the wind I need, I've got the prayers you crave. Let's make a deal.
jokes.setup: Homer praying for wind
jokes.punchline: Treating prayer like a business transaction with God
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Homer's transactional view of religion. Well-structured.

jokes.text: Nothing is ever right for you.
jokes.setup: Marge commenting about too much electricity, then complaining their house isn't bright enough
jokes.punchline: Homer saying the same response to both opposite complaints
jokes.explanation: Good callback structure showing Homer's lazy argumentative style. Perfect character consistency.

jokes.text: If you see a downed power line, do whatever it is people are supposed to do in that situation.
jokes.setup: Marge giving safety advice as kids head out
jokes.punchline: Admitting she doesn't actually know proper safety protocol
jokes.explanation: Relatable parental moment - wanting to give safety advice but not knowing specifics

jokes.text: I'm gonna have my next birthday party on this thing.
jokes.setup: Lisa commenting on the whale's powerlessness
jokes.punchline: Bart's callous plan to use dead whale as party venue
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart insensitivity contrasting with Lisa's empathy. Dark but in character.

jokes.text: Check it out! World's biggest armpit fart.
jokes.setup: Bart discovering the whale's blowhole
jokes.punchline: Reducing majestic whale anatomy to juvenile humor
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart reducing everything to crude humor. Perfectly in character but predictable.

jokes.text: Why should we? / Yeah, where was that whale when my mom moved out?
jokes.setup: Lisa asking townspeople to help the whale
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's non-sequitur complaint about whale not helping with personal problems
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd logic connecting whale rescue to personal family trauma

jokes.text: Never tell a bully you're afraid.
jokes.setup: Milhouse saying he's afraid to answer Nelson's question
jokes.punchline: Immediately violating his own rule by announcing his fear
jokes.explanation: Good self-defeating logic from Milhouse. Structure works well with immediate contradiction.

jokes.text: Man, who are these belts made for, French super models?
jokes.setup: Homer trying to put on tool belt
jokes.punchline: Blaming French supermodels for belt size rather than his own weight
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer deflection from personal responsibility. Oddly specific demographic choice adds humor.

jokes.text: Haven't you learned anything from watching Bart drive? / A little.
jokes.setup: Homer telling Lisa to bring the car around
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to Bart's underage driving as educational
jokes.explanation: Good callback to series history with Homer's casual parenting

jokes.text: It's not like it's stuck in the bottom of a well or in a Mexican prison. But that would make a great movie.
jokes.setup: Homer saying moving whale shouldn't be hard
jokes.punchline: Escalating to increasingly dramatic scenarios then pitching them as entertainment
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from practical to absurd, Homer's movie pitch instinct is perfect character beat

jokes.text: We're busting him out of prison, no matter what you say, Escobar! God, this thing writes itself.
jokes.setup: Homer continuing movie pitch about whale rescue
jokes.punchline: Getting carried away with prison break scenario and commenting on the writing
jokes.explanation: Meta commentary works but feels slightly forced. Good continuation of previous bit.

jokes.text: We'll need the town's strongest men to roll the whale back in the water. / Hmm. We need a better idea.
jokes.setup: Homer's plan to use strong men for whale rescue
jokes.punchline: Visual reveal of Springfield's pathetic strong men (implied)
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff with visual gag of inadequate townspeople

jokes.text: We'll have to find our ten smartest men. / It's hard to believe anyone in that contest can be considered smart.
jokes.setup: Homer's backup plan to use smart men
jokes.punchline: Visual of equally pathetic 'smart' contestants
jokes.explanation: Follow-up gag showing Springfield's general incompetence

jokes.text: It's hard to believe anyone in that contest can be considered smart.
jokes.setup: Town looking for ten smartest men to help with whale
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy's condescending observation while presumably participating
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy superiority complex. Irony of him criticizing while participating.

jokes.text: It's hard to believe anyone in that contest can be considered smart.
jokes.setup: Wiggum observing the intelligence contest
jokes.punchline: Chief Wiggum questioning others' intelligence
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - Chief Wiggum is typically Springfield's least intelligent character

jokes.text: No, no, you're gonna like it. It's about the ocean.
jokes.setup: Homer offering to read poetry to sad Lisa
jokes.punchline: His confident but misguided assumption about what she'd like
jokes.explanation: Homer's well-meaning but clumsy attempt at comfort. Realistic parental moment.

jokes.text: And if there's one thing I can't stand, it's being killed.
jokes.setup: Homer saying it kills him to see Lisa sad
jokes.punchline: Taking the metaphor literally with understated delivery
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer literalism delivered with perfect deadpan timing. Good economy.

jokes.text: If that flattened squirrel we poked last week could only see us now.
jokes.setup: Bart and Milhouse about to poke dead whale
jokes.punchline: Referencing previous dead animal poking as career progression
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about boys' morbid fascination. Good character consistency for Bart.

jokes.text: Could you please stop dynamiting?! Sorry. I sat on the plunger.
jokes.setup: Dynamite going off during whale memorial
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's accidental activation and casual explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chief Wiggum incompetence. Physical comedy with character-appropriate explanation.

jokes.text: Behold, I am Captain Kirk from Star Trek One. Two. Five. Generations. Boston Legal.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy in elaborate costume
jokes.punchline: Listing William Shatner's career chronologically with increasing desperation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Shatner's career decline. Perfect for Comic Book Guy's obsessive knowledge.

jokes.text: so I got you a pet that will never die. An invisible dog.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to replace Lisa's attachment to whale
jokes.punchline: Solution is imaginary pet that can't die because it doesn't exist
jokes.explanation: Homer's logic is both touching and completely absurd

jokes.text: Hey! Not on the rug! You bad dog! Ow! Ow! Ow! Ooh! Ooh! Stop biting my leg!
jokes.setup: Homer pretending to have invisible dog as Lisa's pet
jokes.punchline: Getting genuinely attacked by imaginary pet
jokes.explanation: Homer taking invisible dog bit too far, getting 'attacked' by his own imagination

jokes.text: Finally silenced that mournful wail.
jokes.setup: Homer oiling squeaky door as whale sounds are heard outside
jokes.punchline: Thinking he fixed the 'whale sound' when real whales are calling
jokes.explanation: Excellent dramatic irony - Homer's mundane explanation for something magical. Well-crafted timing.

jokes.text: Typical eco-jerks- using words to talk.
jokes.setup: Environmentalists explaining their position on sharks vs whales
jokes.punchline: Homer's anti-intellectual dismissal taken to absurd extreme
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer anti-intellectualism taken to its logical extreme. Brilliant economy and character voice.

jokes.text: A shark's reptilian brain understands only one thing-- the crime of murder.
jokes.setup: Homer defending his position on sharks
jokes.punchline: Mixing up reptilian/shark taxonomy while being dramatically wrong about shark behavior
jokes.explanation: Homer's confident ignorance about animal behavior and anatomy. Good dramatic delivery.

jokes.text: My diet is working.
jokes.setup: Sharks thinking Homer is a baby whale
jokes.punchline: Homer's delusional positive spin on being mistaken for a whale
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer delusion and optimism. Excellent economy with maximum character revelation.

jokes.text: Why couldn't I be eaten by a giraffe? That would be fun.
jokes.setup: Homer being attacked by sharks
jokes.punchline: Wishing for a more enjoyable form of being eaten alive
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd Homer logic about preferable ways to die. Good character voice.

jokes.text: Ooh, a baby whale. My diet is working.
jokes.setup: Homer surrounded by sharks underwater
jokes.punchline: Misinterpreting shark interest as weight loss success
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic finding positive in life-threatening situation

jokes.text: It'll either make him go away or eat you quicker.
jokes.setup: Giving Homer advice about hitting shark on nose
jokes.punchline: Casually admitting the advice might backfire fatally
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of unhelpful survival advice

jokes.text: Ooh, maybe the blood will scare the sharks off.
jokes.setup: Homer bleeding from shark attack
jokes.punchline: Completely backwards understanding of how blood affects sharks
jokes.explanation: Homer's complete ignorance about basic shark facts. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: He's young, he's got two cute kids. He'll be moving in with a sexy lady octopus in no time.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking if the whale family will be okay
jokes.punchline: Homer projecting human dating scenarios onto marine life
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer anthropomorphizing - applying human relationship patterns to whales. Cross-species romance adds absurdity.

jokes.text: But if I'm not mistaken, there's a little whale-a-puss on the way.
jokes.setup: Homer continuing whale family narrative
jokes.punchline: Made-up word combining whale and octopus for hybrid offspring
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay but the absurd biological impossibility is the real joke. Good callback structure.

tmdb_overview: The Simpsons decide to embrace a cheaper, alternate source of energy by erecting a wind turbine in their backyard. But when Homer realizes some of the power is being directed to the local electric company, he decides to remove his home from the grid and becomes completely dependent upon an unreliable source of power. Meanwhile, a storm erupts, trapping a magnificent 150-foot-long blue whale ashore, and Lisa and Homer attempt to help the poor creature.
